summary: Do not ship: the post-gate CSS refinements violate the explicit reduced-motion acceptance criterion, and the refreshed evidence does not test them.
triage: review-gate SKILL.md rules govern: render EVERY finding as a full table row, the human decides each row (accept/reject/defer/question — free text), echo back before applying, max 2 rounds, append decisions.md and COMMIT it with this artifact.

findings.title: Reduced-motion leaves newly added CSS animations enabled
findings.body: Story 23 requires every new animation to be disabled under reduced motion, but `fieldFocus` now unconditionally adds `transition-colors`, and the new ScrollArea token unconditionally animates scrollbar opacity. The same post-gate batch also adds unconditional color transitions to the profile-name input and collapsible header. Existing reduced-motion checks cover Motion props plus panel/select transitions, so the committed green suite can pass while these new CSS animations remain active. Motion-sensitive users therefore still receive animations despite the release requirement.
findings.recommendation: Condition every CSS transition introduced by this branch on motion preference, for example with `motion-reduce:transition-none`, including `fieldFocus`, `scrollbarTrack`, the profile-name input, and collapsible-header color feedback. Add a Playwright reduced-motion assertion that compares computed transition duration/property for these CSS-driven surfaces, with a normal-motion sensitivity control.

next_steps: Disable the uncovered CSS transitions under `prefers-reduced-motion: reduce`.
Extend smoke coverage to include CSS-driven focus, header-color, and scrollbar transitions.
Refresh the pinned full-suite verification evidence after the fix.
